outlook.headline: Hyperscaler capex remains broadening into 2026Q1
outlook.summary: Company reads are overwhelmingly bullish, with strength spanning AI compute, networking, test, and data-center infrastructure; the mention trend is still elevated at 2.2x and running 11% above the trailing average despite easing from the 2.5x peak. Beneficiaries are the suppliers closest to hyperscaler buildouts and AI silicon ramps, while the only notable lag is Microchip’s neutral read; watch whether current engagement keeps converting into actual hardware pull-through.

drivers.label: AI infrastructure spending stays very strong
drivers.detail: Hyperscaler adoption and capacity expansion remain strong, supporting continued demand across compute and custom silicon exposure.

drivers.label: Build activity is still accelerating
drivers.detail: Multiple reads point to ongoing and accelerating hyperscaler build-outs that are feeding hardware sales, services, and facility-related demand.

drivers.label: Custom silicon, optics, and rack-content ramps
drivers.detail: Engagement across major hyperscalers is translating into wins in interconnect, verification, power, and networking content, implying durable capex intensity.

drivers.label: Test and wafer-fab demand still outruns supply
drivers.detail: Hyperscaler capex is continuing to drive WFE and test demand faster than the industry can add capacity.

risks: Latest mention intensity has slipped from the 2.5x peak, so watch for a near-term fade in hyperscaler momentum.
Conversion from engagement/design wins to volume shipments could slow in custom AI, optics, and power platforms.
Any easing of equipment supply constraints could reduce urgency in WFE and test demand pull-through.
